acceptance.statement: Iteration 11-A passes if it records whether source_digest_reentry_buffer_v1 changes the 11-0 trajectory class, emits the reentry chain records, preserves budget exactness and claim boundaries, and records the next 11-* question.

claim_boundary.reason: A bounded-degrading 12-window endpoint does not justify ID6, identity acceptance, agency, or semantic choice claims.

cultivation.next_question: Can neutral_absorber_reservoir_v1 convert bounded-degrading source-digest reentry into bounded-flat or recovering long-horizon compatibility?
what_this_branch_teaches: Source-digest reentry is enough to beat 11-0 over 12 windows.
Endpoint survival alone is not sufficient because slopes still degrade.
The next branch should test whether neutral absorption can flatten unresolved leakage and support/interference slopes.

arc_of_becoming_interpretation.expressed_property: Serialized source-digest reentry expresses partial recovery: it bounds the 12-window endpoint while preserving a degrading trend.

naturalization.why_not_naturalized: The branch uses a serialized experiment-local reentry policy. It is regime-assisted recovery, not endogenous native formation of the recovery precondition.

observations.interpretation: The buffer reduces unresolved leakage from the 11-0 baseline, but does not flatten it.
The fixed 12-window endpoint passes under this policy.
Support decay is reduced enough to remain inside the horizon threshold, but it still trends downward.
The improvement is not caused by mass creation or budget discontinuity.

arc_of_becoming_interpretation.question: Can source_digest_reentry_buffer_v1 convert the 11-0 trajectory from unbounded degradation into bounded-flat, bounded-improving, or oscillatory-recovering compatibility?

branch_record.next_question: Can neutral_absorber_reservoir_v1 convert bounded-degrading source-digest reentry into bounded-flat or recovering long-horizon compatibility?
branch_record.question_answered: Can source_digest_reentry_buffer_v1 convert the 11-0 trajectory from unbounded degradation into bounded-flat, bounded-improving, or oscillatory-recovering compatibility?
branch_record.trajectory_interpretation: Source-digest reentry changes the no-recovery class from unbounded degradation to bounded degradation over the frozen 12-window horizon. This is real improvement, but the positive leakage/interference slopes mean it is not stable long-term C3 compatibility.
what_was_learned: Source-digest reentry is enough to beat 11-0 over 12 windows.
Endpoint survival alone is not sufficient because slopes still degrade.
The next branch should test whether neutral absorption can flatten unresolved leakage and support/interference slopes.

control_rows.purpose: Keep the 11-0 source failure visible as the baseline.
Reject a reentry buffer that cannot meet the frozen leakage budget.
Reject reentry credited to the wrong basin digest.
Reject fixture-side or report-side route preference.
Reject recovery by mass creation or silent normalization.
Reject ID6 promotion from endpoint pass alone.

long_horizon_candidate_row.becoming_expressed_property: Serialized source-digest reentry expresses partial recovery: it bounds the 12-window endpoint while preserving a degrading trend.

reentry_policy.capture_fraction_derivation: The frozen per-window unresolved leakage budget requires capturing at least 79.1666% of the 0.04 source wrong-basin leakage. 11-A uses the deterministic serialized value 0.8.

series_decision.claim_boundary: No ID6, identity acceptance, RC identity collapse, agency, semantic choice, biological identity, personhood, or unrestricted identity claim is opened by 11-A.
series_decision.next_branch_question: Can neutral_absorber_reservoir_v1 convert bounded-degrading source-digest reentry into bounded-flat or recovering long-horizon compatibility?
series_decision.reason: 11-A improves the baseline and passes the frozen 12-window endpoint, but it remains bounded-degrading. The 11-* series needs another branch before closeout.

trajectory.trajectory_interpretation: Source-digest reentry changes the no-recovery class from unbounded degradation to bounded degradation over the frozen 12-window horizon. This is real improvement, but the positive leakage/interference slopes mean it is not stable long-term C3 compatibility.
